title: Note reconstruction: SpendableNote DB row → sapling::Note
description:
Implement spendable_note_to_sapling(row: &SpendableNote) -> Result<sapling::Note, TxBuildError>.

A sapling::Note requires: recipient PaymentAddress (diversifier g_d + pk_d), value NoteValue, and Rseed (or old rcm). All three are stored in the new note_g_d / note_pk_d / note_rseed / note_rseed_after_zip212 columns from migration v6.

Steps:
1. Spawn a research subagent to find sapling::Note construction API — specifically Note::from_parts(recipient, value, rseed) or equivalent in ~/.cargo/git/checkouts/sapling-crypto-*/
2. Construct PaymentAddress from g_d (11 bytes) and pk_d (32 bytes): sapling::keys::PaymentAddress::from_bytes or equivalent
3. Construct Rseed from rseed bytes: Rseed::AfterZip212(bytes) or Rseed::BeforeZip212(rcm)
4. Construct sapling::value::NoteValue from value_zatoshi (u64)
5. Construct sapling::Note from parts

Defensive rules:
- PaymentAddress::from_bytes returning None → TxBuildError::NoteDeserialize('invalid payment address in stored note — DB may be corrupt')
- Any invalid byte length → TxBuildError::NoteDeserialize with the column name and actual length
- value_zatoshi overflow when converting to NoteValue → TxBuildError::NoteDeserialize

Tests (oracle: derive expected PaymentAddress bytes from known DFVK + diversifier index, verify round-trip):
- Construct a known sapling::Note from known key material, extract g_d/pk_d/rseed, reconstruct via spendable_note_to_sapling, verify value and address match
- NULL-equivalent bytes (all zeros) for g_d → TxBuildError::NoteDeserialize
- Wrong length for pk_d → TxBuildError::NoteDeserialize
- value_zatoshi = u64::MAX → must succeed (NoteValue supports full range) or return clear error
acceptance_criteria: round-trip test passes with known key material; all invalid-input paths return TxBuildError; no panics on any input
